old_claim.note: Advanced Stone Tools is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No advanced-stone-tool dependency remains because the evidenced claim is chemical plant-extract tanning and the study does not identify a required stone implement.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract attributes the tanning evidence to plant and lipid biomarkers recovered through optical, molecular, and isotopic analysis.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped innovation is an aqueous plant treatment identified chemically in worked leather rather than a stone-tool manufacturing technique.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Mechanical hide preparation is inherited through leather_working, while no particular advanced stone toolkit is necessary for the chemical stage.

ontology_before: A specific tanning chemistry inherited a generic stone-age chronology edge.
ontology_after: The node depends on broader leather working and its directly evidenced plant treatment only.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: leather_tanning no longer directly depends on advanced_stone_tools.
would_reject_if: The Schnidejoch study identified an advanced stone implement as necessary to produce the analyzed leather.
short_reason: No advanced stone tool is evidenced as part of the plant-tanning process.
